text: sdhDetection() function allows automatically detect report every episode CGM-detected hypoglycaemia. function takes CGM data returns participant episodes hypoglycaemia detected: start end time episode, duration, nadir day/night status (night: 00h00-06h00). episode overlaps day night status, marked overlap. default hypoglycaemia detection limit 3.9 mmol/L duration 15 minutes. can see table P02 experienced 7 episodes hypoglycaemia 3.9 mmol/L minimum duration 15 minutes. P01 experience hypoglycaemia episodes characteristics. default parameters can changed explore different glucose threshold, minimum duration. example: changing default parameters, see P01 experienced one episode hypoglycaemia 3 mmol/L duration 10 minutes. sleep data available, user can also sleep information output changing default AddSleepStatus parameter “yes”. run, CGM dataset used must include sleep_status column (can obtained using cgmsleepLink() function). example:

text: FileType argument function set "wpai", function calculate WPAI scores based raw data. scores give insight impact hypoglycaemia work activity. what: 4. Linking Real-Time and Retrospective uMotif Person-Reported Hypoglycaemia (PRH) Data
title: Person-Reported Hypoglycaemia Data
text: prhLink() function allows linkage real-time (motif flower) retrospective (check-ins) uMotif PRH data. user firstly needs clean umotif data using uMotifClean() function, shown : resulting output dataset one row per PRH event. real-time retrospective PRH recorded within one hour , appear row considered event. , instances. night_status column indicates whether PRH occurred night (00:00 06:00) day. output contains additional information hypoglycaemia symptoms reported, illustrative purposes , limited number columns shown. user sleep data available, possible add information dataset running function changing default parameters, shown . create additional variable sleep_status PRH defined using sleep tracker (Fitbit).
code: ## Cleaning motif and checkin data  motif <- umotifClean(DataFrame = hypometrics::raw_motif_segment,                      FileType = "motif") checkin <- umotifClean(DataFrame = hypometrics::raw_checkin,                        FileType = "checkin")  ## Creating the linked PRH dataset prhLink(MotifDataFrame = motif,         CheckinDataFrame = checkin) %>%   dplyr::select(1:4) %>%   dplyr::slice(1:10) #>         id checkin_prh_timestamp motif_prh_timestamp night_status #>     <char>                <POSc>              <POSc>       <char> #>  1:    P01                  <NA> 2026-01-01 12:22:00          Day #>  2:    P01   2026-01-07 04:08:00                <NA>        Night #>  3:    P01   2026-01-12 04:01:00                <NA>        Night #>  4:    P01   2026-01-12 22:17:00                <NA>          Day #>  5:    P01                  <NA> 2026-01-15 07:02:00          Day #>  6:    P02   2026-01-02 02:21:00                <NA>        Night #>  7:    P02                  <NA> 2026-01-02 19:25:00          Day #>  8:    P02                  <NA> 2026-01-02 19:30:00          Day #>  9:    P02                  <NA> 2026-01-02 19:35:00          Day #> 10:    P02   2026-01-03 00:50:00                <NA>        Night ## Creating the linked PRH dataset prhLink(MotifDataFrame = motif,         CheckinDataFrame = checkin,         AddSleepStatus = "yes",         SleepDataFrame = raw_sleep)

title: Methods To Analyse Continuous Glucose Monitoring, Activity, Sleep and Person-Reported Hypoglycaemia Data
text: building blocks now hypometrics package developed effort support facilitate analysis data Hypo-METRICS (Hypoglycaemia - MEasurement, ThResholds ImpaCtS) study. Hypo-METRICS multinational, multicentre, observational study conducted 2020 2022 part Hypo-RESOLVE project investigate hypoglycaemia impact diabetes. 600 participants type 1 insulin-treated type 2 diabetes recruited across 9 UK EU sites. 10 weeks, participants wore CGM Fitbit recorded episodes hypoglycaemia using Hypo-METRICS smartphone app. can find information protocols Hypo-METRICS study Hypo-METRICS app.

code: cgm <- hypometrics::cgmInterpolate(   raw_cgm,   Interpolate = TRUE,   MinGap      = 600,   # only act when gaps are at least 10 minutes   Granularity = 300,    # 5-minute target grid  <-- IMPORTANT   MaxGap      = 1800   # interpolate gaps < 30 minutes; leave >= 30 as NA )

title: Simulated interpolated CGM Dataset — cgm
text: dataset generated hypometrics::cgmInterpolate() follows: Input: [`raw_cgm`]. Explicit gaps: Gaps first made explicit         transforming target grid (5‑minute). Interpolation: Linear interpolation applied gaps         whose duration strictly less 30 minutes         (MaxGap = 1800 seconds). Long gaps: Gaps ≥ 30 minutes         interpolated; glucose remains NA. Granularity: target resolution 5 minutes         (Granularity = 300 seconds). procedure retains two synthetic participants [`raw_cgm`] (IDs "P01", "P02"), distinct profiles, hypoglycaemia patterns, providing regularised 5‑minute time series convenient summaries, visualisation, episode detection.

title: Implicit to Explicit Missingness and Linear Interpolation of Glucose Data. — cgmInterpolate
text: DataFrame dataframe CGM data filled interpolated specified. Must contain columns: id, cgm_timestamp glucose. Interpolate Logical value (TRUE/FALSE) determines whether linear interpolation conducted. FALSE, implicit gaps CGM timestamps turned explicit gaps changes data made.D Default TRUE linear interpolation conducted. MinGap Numeric value defines minimum duration seconds gaps filled interpolated. Default 120 seconds, .e. 2 minutes MaxGap Numeric value defines maximum duration seconds gaps filled interpolated. Default 1800 seconds, .e. 30 mines Granularity Numeric value defines granularity Glucose data desired seconds. Typically closely linked MinGap parameter e.g. granularity data needed 60 seconds, MinGap 120 gap wide enough add extra CGM timestamps. Default 60 seconds 1 min. .e. resulting data frame CGM data 1 -min level.

what: Simulated EQ‑5D‑5L Health Status Dataset — raw_eq5d5l
title: Simulated EQ‑5D‑5L Health Status Dataset — raw_eq5d5l
text: `raw_eq5d5l` contains fully synthetic version EQ‑5D‑5L instrument two simulated participants (`P01` `P02`) across three study stages: "Registration", "Day-7", "Day-14". dataset includes ISO‑8601 timestamped assessments categorical responses five EQ‑5D‑5L dimensions.

title: Simulated EQ‑5D‑5L Health Status Dataset — raw_eq5d5l
Simulated EQ‑5D‑5L Health Status Dataset — raw_eq5d5l
text: tibble following variables: userid Character participant ID ("P01", "P02"). stage Assessment stage ("Registration", "Day-7", "Day-14"). localTimestamp Character ISO‑8601 timestamp including milliseconds timezone. SC Self‑Care domain level ("SC1"–"SC5"). AD Anxiety/Depression domain level ("AD1"–"AD5"). UA Usual Activities domain level ("UA1"–"UA5"). MB Mobility domain level ("MB1"–"MB5"). PD Pain/Discomfort domain level ("PD1"–"PD5").

title: Simulated EQ‑5D‑5L Health Status Dataset — raw_eq5d5l
text: dataset simulated intended demonstrating workflows examples within `hypometrics` package. Participants timing "P01" — Registration time: "2026-01-01 07:17:00" (UTC) "P02" — Registration time: "2026-01-02 15:52:00" (UTC) Follow‑assessments occur exactly **7** **14** days Registration participant. EQ‑5D‑5L dimensions (5 levels) EQ‑5D‑5L domain represented using 5‑level structure, encoded : SC1–SC5: **Self‑Care** AD1–AD5: **Anxiety/Depression** UA1–UA5: **Usual Activities** MB1–MB5: **Mobility** PD1–PD5: **Pain / Discomfort** Generation script dataset produced reproducibly using:

title: Simulated EQ‑5D‑5L Health Status Dataset — raw_eq5d5l
code: data(raw_eq5d5l, package = "hypometrics")  # Inspect structure dplyr::glimpse(raw_eq5d5l) #> Rows: 6 #> Columns: 8 #> $ userid         <chr> "P01", "P01", "P01", "P02", "P02", "P02" #> $ stage          <chr> "Day-14", "Day-7", "Registration", "Day-14", "Day-7", "… #> $ localTimestamp <chr> "2026-01-15T07:17:00.000+00:00", "2026-01-08T07:17:00.0… #> $ SC             <chr> "SC5", "SC3", "SC1", "SC1", "SC4", "SC5" #> $ AD             <chr> "AD2", "AD2", "AD1", "AD2", "AD3", "AD5" #> $ UA             <chr> "UA3", "UA3", "UA2", "UA4", "UA4", "UA2" #> $ MB             <chr> "MB4", "MB5", "MB3", "MB2", "MB4", "MB5" #> $ PD             <chr> "PD1", "PD2", "PD4", "PD3", "PD2", "PD1"  # Show values for P01 raw_eq5d5l %>% dplyr::filter(userid == "P01") #> # A tibble: 3 × 8 #>   userid stage        localTimestamp               SC    AD    UA    MB    PD    #>   <chr>  <chr>        <chr>                        <chr> <chr> <chr> <chr> <chr> #> 1 P01    Day-14       2026-01-15T07:17:00.000+00:… SC5   AD2   UA3   MB4   PD1   #> 2 P01    Day-7        2026-01-08T07:17:00.000+00:… SC3   AD2   UA3   MB5   PD2   #> 3 P01    Registration 2026-01-01T07:17:00.000+00:… SC1   AD1   UA2   MB3   PD4    # Summarise EQ-5D-5L domains by participant and stage raw_eq5d5l %>%   dplyr::group_by(userid, stage) %>%   dplyr::summarise(     across(c(SC, AD, UA, MB, PD), ~ dplyr::n_distinct(.x)),     .groups = "drop"   ) #> # A tibble: 6 × 7 #>   userid stage           SC    AD    UA    MB    PD #>   <chr>  <chr>        <int> <int> <int> <int> <int> #> 1 P01    Day-14           1     1     1     1     1 #> 2 P01    Day-7            1     1     1     1     1 #> 3 P01    Registration     1     1     1     1     1 #> 4 P02    Day-14           1     1     1     1     1 #> 5 P02    Day-7            1     1     1     1     1 #> 6 P02    Registration     1     1     1     1     1

title: Simulated Fitbit‑Style Heart Rate Data — raw_hr
text: dataset designed demonstration testing HR‑related processing visualisation tools within `hypometrics` package. dataset includes: Participants "P01" — HR start: "2026‑01‑01 07:17:00" (UTC) "P02" — HR start: "2026‑01‑02 15:52:00" (UTC) Simulation features 1‑minute HR sampling 14 days per participant Circadian rhythm (afternoon HR peak, overnight depression) Sleep HR lowering (smaller noise, lower baseline) Stochastic noise resembling optical HR sensors Exercise bouts HR rise → plateau → recovery Fitbit‑style missingness:         1–2 minute random gaps, occasional long dropouts NA heart rate HR values rounded whole‑number bpm Relation CGM data HR timeline aligned simulated CGM dataset [`raw_cgm`]: participant, HR starts exactly CGM monitoring begins continues two‑week duration. title: Simulated Minute‑Level Step Count Data — raw_step
text: `raw_step` contains **synthetic Fitbit‑style step count data** two simulated participants (`P01` `P02`). dataset spans **two weeks** **1‑minute resolution**. includes **exercise sessions**, **sleep‑related inactivity**, **Fitbit‑like missingness patterns**, providing realistic structure testing algorithms process wearable activity data.

title: Simulated Minute‑Level Step Count Data — raw_step
Simulated Minute‑Level Step Count Data — raw_step
text: tibble three columns: id Character participant ID ("P01", "P02"). step_timestamp POSIXct timestamp UTC, 1‑minute resolution. count Whole‑number step count per minute.

title: Simulated Minute‑Level Step Count Data — raw_step
text: Participants "P01" — starts "2026‑01‑01 07:17:00" (UTC) "P02" — starts "2026‑01‑02 15:52:00" (UTC) participant exactly **14 days** simulated minute‑level data. Activity patterns Daytime activity: mild random movement realistic variability. Sleep inactivity: step counts drop near‑zero 23:00–06:00. Exercise sessions: intermittent bouts ramp‑,     sustained high counts, gradual cooldown. Noise: stochastic variation representing device behavioural variation. Missingness model (Fitbit‑like) Step counts include NA values introduced using: tiny micro‑gaps (1–2 minute sensor losses), longer dropouts (10–60 minutes). patterns mimic real‑world wrist‑worn accelerometer behaviour motion artifacts, device removal, Bluetooth disconnection. Generation script dataset produced reproducibly script:

title: Simulated Minute‑Level Step Count Data — raw_step
code: data(raw_step, package = "hypometrics")  # Structure dplyr::glimpse(raw_step) #> Rows: 40,320 #> Columns: 3 #> $ id             <chr> "P01", "P01", "P01", "P01", "P01", "P01", "P01", "P01",… #> $ step_timestamp <dttm> 2026-01-01 07:17:00, 2026-01-01 07:18:00, 2026-01-01 0… #> $ count          <dbl> 35, 38, 52, 44, 17, 46, 44, 42, 35, 37, 29, 46, 35, 34,…  # First few rows for P01 dplyr::filter(raw_step, id == "P01") %>% head() #> # A tibble: 6 × 3 #>   id    step_timestamp      count #>   <chr> <dttm>              <dbl> #> 1 P01   2026-01-01 07:17:00    35 #> 2 P01   2026-01-01 07:18:00    38 #> 3 P01   2026-01-01 07:19:00    52 #> 4 P01   2026-01-01 07:20:00    44 #> 5 P01   2026-01-01 07:21:00    17 #> 6 P01   2026-01-01 07:22:00    46  # Missingness percentage per participant raw_step %>%   dplyr::group_by(id) %>%   dplyr::summarise(     pct_missing = mean(is.na(count)) * 100   ) #> # A tibble: 2 × 2 #>   id    pct_missing #>   <chr>       <dbl> #> 1 P01          3.72 #> 2 P02          4.49
